content:
Welcome back!
You’re receiving this note because you are currently registered for at least one of my classes this semester:
 IMS211 (Introduction to Game Studies); IMS212 (Introduction to Game Design); IMS384 (Rhetoric of Games).  IMS211, IMS212 My sections of IMS211 and IMS212 are both “hybrid synchronous” courses. For our purposes, that means that you have a choice:
  Choice A: Twice weekly, at the appointed hour, from your dorm room, your apartment, your RV, or even your pool, you will join us for class. We will meet (typically) via Zoom or Google Meet.
  Choice B: At the same appointed hour, on the same appointed days, if you wish, you may attend class in-person.
  For the purposes of course requirements and grading, Choice A and Choice B are equivalent and interchangeable. You may stay remote the entire semester without penalty; you may attend in person the entire semester without penalty; you may mix and match without penalty. You do not need to inform me of your choice on a daily basis, nor do you need to tell me about a change in your plans halfway through the semester.
First Class Meetings, 211+212 These courses meet for the first time on Tuesday (tomorrow, 26 January).
If you want to meet in person, here are the assigned classrooms for IMS211B, IMS212B, and IMS212C.
 IMS211B Game Studies meets on Tues+Thurs, 115PM-235PM, 009 Benton Hall IMS212B Game Design meets on Tues+Thurs, 1005AM-1125AM, 201 Laws Hall IMS212C Game Design meets on Tues+Thurs, 1140AM-100PM, 201 Laws Hall
 If you will be joining the class virtually, check your email early tomorrow AM for a link to our virtual classroom (via Zoom).
 IMS384 The Rhetoric of Games course, IMS384, is categorized as “online synchronous” for reasons that are too baroque to explore here. As it stands, that means that there is no in-person course component. We are slated to meet exclusively online, synchronously, at the appointed hour on the appointed days.
However, if I am able to reliably reserve a classroom at least one day a week, IMS384 will also have an in-person option, like the other “hybrid synchronous” courses, above. As is the case with IMS211 and IMS212, of course, attending in-person will always be strictly optional: In-person attendance will never be a requirement.
First Class Meeting, IMS384 IMS384 is currently online-only, Wed and Fri 250PM-410PM. In the event I can get a classroom for IMS384, I will apprise you of the location by Tuesday evening. In any event, I will share a link to our Zoom classroom space with everyone enrolled early Wednesday AM.
 Online Participation Broadly, then, one last salient point: Given that 2020 was largely something out of a John Carpenter movie, I took a very laissez-faire approach to online participation. Students were not required to appear on-camera during classes, and – not knowing the particulars of anyone’s situation – I was usually loathe to demand students participate in class discussions, etc.. My intentions were good, but the outcomes were not.
Therefore, as a rule, when you attend class remotely, I fully expect that you will be on-camera and audible for the duration of each class session. To that end, our shared time together this semester is mostly dedicated to (1) in-class discussions, (2) small group work, and (3) occasional student presentations; my lectures and interviews will be available to consume outside of our class meetings.
Additional Notes There are lots of details and conversations to have regarding these courses, but I will reserve those for our first meetings on Tuesday and Wednesday.
 Please note that the canvas accounts associated with these classes are currently switched off. I will turn each of them on during our first meeting together, so that we can review the curriculum and schedule on Canvas together, as the Good Lord intended.
 I look forward to seeing you soon.
Best,
Prof. Garrison LeMasters CCA | ETBD | G+S Miami Oxford Ohio

title: Welcome Friends
